@graph.name: AC Maintenance & Tune-Ups in Fort Lauderdale
@graph.description: Professional twice-a-year AC tune-up and maintenance service for Fort Lauderdale and Broward County homes — coil cleaning, refrigerant and electrical checks, condensate drain clearing, and a 20-point inspection built for South Florida heat, humidity, and salt air.

mainEntity.name: How often should you service your AC in Florida?

acceptedAnswer.text: Twice a year — spring and fall. Florida systems run nearly year-round and battle humidity and salt air, so a single annual service isn't enough to prevent peak-summer breakdowns in Fort Lauderdale and Broward County.

mainEntity.name: What does an AC tune-up include?

acceptedAnswer.text: Coil cleaning, refrigerant and electrical checks, capacitor testing, condensate drain clearing, filter and airflow inspection, and thermostat calibration — plus a written report and a cooling temperature-split test.

mainEntity.name: How much does an AC tune-up cost in Fort Lauderdale?

acceptedAnswer.text: A standard AC tune-up in the Fort Lauderdale and Broward County area runs about $79–$149. Maintenance-plan members typically pay less per visit and get priority scheduling and repair discounts.

mainEntity.name: What is included in AC maintenance service in Broward County?

acceptedAnswer.text: Recurring scheduled care — typically two visits a year combining a full tune-up with proactive part inspection, refrigerant top-off when needed, drain-line treatment, and efficiency optimization to reduce breakdowns and extend system life.

mainEntity.name: Is an AC maintenance plan worth it in Broward County?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Two visits a year reduce breakdowns, lower energy bills, extend equipment life, and satisfy warranty documentation requirements — usually paying for itself within a season or two in Florida's climate.

mainEntity.name: Can a tune-up prevent emergency AC repairs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Largely, yes. Most summer failures trace back to dirty coils, weak capacitors, low refrigerant, or clogged condensate drains — all of which a professional tune-up catches and corrects early.
